hateful_detection_parsed.description: The image displays a person wearing KKK regalia and a Nazi armband, which are hate symbols associated with racism and white supremacy. The text explicitly expresses hatred based on race ("i hate when i see a black") while ironically claiming to perform a benevolent act, conveying a message of racial intolerance and dehumanization.

diffusion_prompt_raw:
{
  "hate_source": "The image depicts a Ku Klux Klan member, and the text explicitly expresses racism against Black people while attempting to frame the hate as a 'kind' gesture.",
  "hate_location": "INTERSECTIONAL",
  "severity": "STRUCTURAL",
  "original_text": "i hate when i see a black out in my neighborhood but i still offer my neighbors a candle",
  "replacement_text": "i hate when the HOA bans candles on the lawn but i still offer my neighbors a candle",
  "strategy": "Transform the image into a parody where a person dressed as a friendly ghost (preserving the white hood/costume shape but removing the hate context) holds up a finger to signal 'shh' or a secret. This keeps the 'white figure with a hood' visual pun but changes the context from a hate group to a spooky/harmless character, aligning with the joke about secretly using candles.",
  "flux_prompt": "Transform the image into a humorous parody of the visual format. The main character should remain a figure in a white hooded costume, but styled as a friendly, cartoonish ghost character (like Casper) rather than a KKK member. The character's hand should be raised towards the camera making a 'shushing' gesture (finger to lips). The character should hold a small tea light candle in their hand. The background should be a suburban neighborhood with police officers looking confused in the distance. Keep the original composition, angle, and lighting, but remove the red cross pin and change the hood style to be rounded and soft like a ghost costume.",
  "expected_change": "The image will retain the 'white figure with a hood' and 'hand gesture' composition but be recontextualized as a friendly ghost character secretly using a candle, removing all hate symbols and allowing the text to be a self-referential joke about HOA rules."
}
